slides.title: The pigment linked to a *sharper older brain*.
slides.sub: They're called anthocyanins — the deep pigments in blueberries and dark berries — and they're tied to slower memory decline.

slides.title: Color is the clue
slides.body: *Anthocyanins* are the pigments that make berries deep blue and purple — and they're powerful antioxidants.

slides.title: Slower memory decline
slides.body: Studies link regular berry eaters to *slower memory decline* and better brain aging over time.

slides.title: A handful most days
slides.body: Research often uses about *half a cup of berries* daily — fresh or frozen both count.

slides.title: Less inflammation up top
slides.body: Anthocyanins may ease *oxidative stress* in the brain, the wear-and-tear tied to aging.

slides.title: Frozen is your friend
slides.body: Frozen berries are cheap, last for months, and keep their anthocyanins. *Toss a handful in* daily.

slides.title: Eat now for a *sharper future*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your meals and habits into one daily Health Score — so brain-healthy choices add up over time.

caption:
The color in your berries isn't just pretty — it may help your brain age slower. 🫐

Anthocyanins are tied to slower memory decline, research often uses about half a cup a day, and frozen counts just as much as fresh. Save this for your shopping list. 🔖

Vibe Health turns daily choices like this into one Health Score that adds up → link in bio.
